title: cephalotaxales
intro: The Cephalotaxales is an order of flowering plants, characterized by their unique characteristics such as having a distinctive arrangement of stamens and a woody or herbaceous habit. This order includes a diverse range of plant families, such as the Cephalotaxaceae, Taxaceae and others. Some notable families within the Cephalotaxales order include the Cephalotaxaceae, which is known to include species such as the Plum Yew. Other examples of plant families within the Cephalotaxales order include the Taxaceae and the Podocarpaceae.

families.family_desc: Cephalotaxaceae is a family of coniferous evergreen shrubs and small trees, commonly known as plum yews, native to East Asia.
